title: penstemon incanus
intro: Penstemon incanus, commonly known as the "Gypsum Beardtongue," is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
